role_summary: Civil Rights History Project interviewee; the LoC oral history is the primary archival source for this catalog page.
ai_reading: Frankye Adams-Johnson's top semantic neighbor is Charles McLaurin (entry 17, count 128); the broader cluster includes Euvester Simpson (#41), Geraldine Crawford Bennett, Toni Breaux, and Willie Elliot Jenkins (#46), Ladners joint interview (Dorie Ann Ladner + Joyce Ann Ladner) (#75).
biographical_paragraph: Frankye Adams-Johnson's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 43 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. The LoC transcript carries a not-auditable audit tier: the catalog cannot independently certify the transcript against primary sources for this entry, and any specific biographical or quoted claims should be confirmed against external archives before citation. Independent biographical sourcing for an extended orientation paragraph is pending; the strongest signal currently available is the embedding pattern named in the AI's reading above.
